channel.title: Varol Cagdas Tok - Technical Articles
channel.description: Technical articles on cybersecurity, software development, and machine learning

item.title: An Introduction to Machine Learning: A Journey Through Its History and Ideas
item.description: A high-level introduction to machine learning, its philosophical underpinnings, and its historical development from early statistical methods to modern deep learning.

item.title: The Perceptron: A Glimpse into the Dawn of Machine Learning
item.description: An introduction to the Perceptron algorithm, one of the earliest and most foundational models in machine learning, covering its architecture, learning rule, and historical significance.

item.title: A Refresher on Linear Algebra: The Language of Machine Learning
item.description: A review of the fundamental concepts of linear algebra—vectors, matrices, and their operations—that form the mathematical language of machine learning.

item.title: Linear Regression: The Workhorse of Machine Learning
item.description: An in-depth look at linear regression, from the method of least squares to the importance of regularization and its role in understanding causality.

item.title: Basis Functions: Transforming Non-Linear Problems into Linear Ones
item.description: An exploration of basis functions and their role in transforming non-linear problems into linear ones, enabling the use of linear models for complex tasks.

item.title: Understanding Function Approximation and the Curse of Dimensionality in Machine Learning
item.description: An exploration of function approximation as a framework for understanding machine learning and the critical challenge of the Curse of Dimensionality.

item.title: Kernel Methods: The Power of Infinite Dimensions and the Kernel Trick
item.description: An exploration of kernel methods, their relationship to basis functions, and their ability to handle high-dimensional data through the kernel trick.

item.title: A Refresher on Probability: The Foundation of Uncertainty in Machine Learning
item.description: A review of the fundamental concepts of probability theory—from random variables to Bayes' theorem—that are essential for understanding machine learning.

item.title: Frequentist vs. Bayesian Statistics: Two Perspectives on Inference and Learning
item.description: An exploration of the philosophical and practical differences between Frequentist and Bayesian statistics, and their implications for machine learning.

item.title: An Introduction to Neural Networks: The Power of Learned Representations
item.description: An introduction to neural networks, covering their architecture, the backpropagation algorithm, and key concepts like regularization.

item.title: An Introduction to Linear Classifiers: Drawing the Line in Data
item.description: An exploration of linear classifiers and the different approaches to building them, including generative models, logistic regression, and classification via regression.

item.title: Support Vector Machines: Finding the Optimal Margin
item.description: An introduction to the Support Vector Machine (SVM), a powerful classification algorithm based on the principle of maximizing the margin between classes.

item.title: Model Selection and Estimating Generalization Performance
item.description: An overview of model selection, the estimation of generalization error, and the theoretical frameworks that guide the process.

item.title: An Introduction to Deep Learning: From Revival to Revolution
item.description: An overview of the fundamental principles of deep learning, exploring the key ingredients that contribute to its success and the theoretical concepts that explain its power.

item.title: Modeling Sequential Data: From Recurrent Neural Networks to the Attention Revolution
item.description: An exploration of architectures designed for sequential data, from Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) to the powerful attention mechanisms that underpin modern models like the Transformer.

item.title: Modeling Sequential Data: From Recurrent Neural Networks to the Attention Revolution
item.description: An exploration of architectures designed for sequential data, from Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) to the powerful attention mechanisms that underpin modern models like the Transformer.

item.title: Manifold Learning, Autoencoders, and Generative Models: Uncovering the Intrinsic Structure of Data
item.description: An exploration of manifold learning, autoencoders, and generative models, and how they are used to uncover the intrinsic structure of data.

item.title: A Guide to Black-Box Testing: Equivalence, Boundary, and Decision Table Techniques
item.description: A look at black-box testing, a method where test cases are derived from the external specification without considering the internal code structure.

item.title: The Test Oracle Problem: How Do You Know Your Test *Really* Passed?
item.description: A test input is only half of a test case. The other half is the oracle. This post explores the challenge of determining the correct output for a given test.

item.title: Measuring Test Suite Quality: A Guide to Coverage Criteria and Mutation Testing
item.description: How do you assess the quality of a test suite? This post covers adequacy criteria, from statement and branch coverage to mutation testing.
